executive_summary: Security assessment of scanme.nmap.org and nmap.org infrastructure revealed multiple critical vulnerabilities including password reset token theft, server-side request forgery allowing internal network access, and widespread open redirects. The absence of HTTPS on scanme.nmap.org and unencrypted FTP services further expose the infrastructure to data interception and credential theft.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing SPF Record
vulnerabilities.description: No SPF record found — domain can be used for email spoofing
vulnerabilities.evidence: No TXT record with 'v=spf1' found for scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add an SPF TXT record (e.g., 'v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all')

vulnerabilities.title: Missing DMARC Record
vulnerabilities.description: No DMARC record found — no email authentication enforcement
vulnerabilities.evidence: No TXT record at _dmarc.scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add a DMARC TXT record at _dmarc.{domain}

vulnerabilities.title: Risky service on port 21 (FTP)
vulnerabilities.description: FTP allows unencrypted file transfer
vulnerabilities.evidence: Port 21 is open on scanme.nmap.org (45.33.32.156)

vulnerabilities.title: No HTTPS / SSL Not Available
vulnerabilities.description: Port 443 is closed or not serving SSL/TLS on scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.evidence: [WinError 10061] No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it
vulnerabilities.remediation: Deploy a TLS certificate and redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Strict-Transport-Security header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing HSTS header allows downgrade attacks and cookie hijacking
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains' header

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing CSP allows XSS and data injection attacks
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'Content-Security-Policy' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement Content-Security-Policy header with strict directives

vulnerabilities.title: Missing X-Frame-Options header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing X-Frame-Options allows clickjacking attacks
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'X-Frame-Options' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'X-Frame-Options: DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' header

vulnerabilities.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options allows MIME-type sniffing
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff' header

vulnerabilities.title: Missing X-XSS-Protection header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing X-XSS-Protection header (legacy but still useful)
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'X-XSS-Protection' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block' header

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Referrer-Policy header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing Referrer-Policy can leak sensitive URLs to third parties
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'Referrer-Policy' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add 'Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin' header

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Permissions-Policy header
vulnerabilities.description: Missing Permissions-Policy allows unrestricted browser feature access
vulnerabilities.evidence: Header 'Permissions-Policy' not found in response from http://scanme.nmap.org
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy header to restrict browser features

vulnerabilities.title: Information disclosure via Server header
vulnerabilities.description: Server reveals technology info: Server: Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu)
vulnerabilities.evidence: Server: Apache/2.4.7 (Ubuntu)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove or suppress the 'Server' header

vulnerabilities.description: FTP allows unencrypted file transfer

vulnerabilities.title: Internal URLs exposed in JavaScript (3 found)
vulnerabilities.description: Internal/private URLs found: https://localhost.twitter.com:8444, https://localhost.twitter.com, https://localhost.twitter.com:8445
vulnerabilities.evidence:
https://localhost.twitter.com:8444
https://localhost.twitter.com
https://localhost.twitter.com:8445
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove references to internal infrastructure from client-side code.

vulnerabilities.title: Open Redirect via 'url' parameter
vulnerabilities.description: Server redirects to attacker-controlled URL via 'url' parameter
vulnerabilities.evidence: Redirect to: https://nmap.org/?url=//evil.com
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate redirect URLs against a whitelist of allowed domains.

vulnerabilities.title: SSRF via 'url' parameter (localhost IPv4)
vulnerabilities.description: Server-Side Request Forgery detected in parameter 'url'. The server made a request to http://127.0.0.1 when injected via 'url'. Type: localhost IPv4.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
URL: http://nmap.org
Parameter: url
Payload: http://127.0.0.1
Indicator: Localhost content indicator: 'apache'
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate and sanitize all URL inputs. Use an allowlist of permitted domains/IPs. Block requests to private IP ranges (127.0.0.0/8, 10.0.0.0/8, 169.254.169.254, etc.). Disable unnecessary URL schemes.

vulnerabilities.title: SSRF via 'link' parameter (localhost IPv4)

vulnerabilities.title: Host Header Injection: Host Override
vulnerabilities.description: The server reflects or processes the injected host header 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' when using Host Override. This can enable password reset poisoning, web cache poisoning, or access to internal resources.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
Test: Host Override
URL: http://nmap.org
Indicator: Evil host 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' reflected in response body
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Host Header Injection: X-Original-URL
vulnerabilities.description: The server reflects or processes the injected host header 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' when using X-Original-URL. This can enable password reset poisoning, web cache poisoning, or access to internal resources.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
Test: X-Original-URL
URL: http://nmap.org
Indicator: Path override returned status 301 (expected 404)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Host Header Injection: X-Rewrite-URL
vulnerabilities.description: The server reflects or processes the injected host header 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' when using X-Rewrite-URL. This can enable password reset poisoning, web cache poisoning, or access to internal resources.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
Test: X-Rewrite-URL
URL: http://nmap.org
Indicator: Path override returned status 301 (expected 404)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Password Reset Poisoning via Host Header
vulnerabilities.description: Password reset endpoint http://nmap.org/reset-password uses the Host header to generate the reset link. An attacker can poison the Host to steal reset tokens.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
URL: http://nmap.org/reset-password
Injected Host: evil.vulnhunter-test.com
Indicator: Reset page reflects poisoned host 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com'
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use a hardcoded base URL for password reset links, not the Host header.

vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Host Header Injection: X-Original-URL
vulnerabilities.description: The server reflects or processes the injected host header 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' when using X-Original-URL. This can enable password reset poisoning, web cache poisoning, or access to internal resources.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
Test: X-Original-URL
URL: https://nmap.org
Indicator: Path override returned status 200 (expected 404)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Host Header Injection: X-Rewrite-URL
vulnerabilities.description: The server reflects or processes the injected host header 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com' when using X-Rewrite-URL. This can enable password reset poisoning, web cache poisoning, or access to internal resources.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
Test: X-Rewrite-URL
URL: https://nmap.org
Indicator: Path override returned status 200 (expected 404)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate the Host header against a whitelist of expected hostnames. Ignore X-Forwarded-Host unless from a trusted reverse proxy. Use absolute URLs with a configured base domain for password reset links.

vulnerabilities.title: Password Reset Poisoning via Host Header
vulnerabilities.description: Password reset endpoint https://nmap.org/reset-password uses the Host header to generate the reset link. An attacker can poison the Host to steal reset tokens.
vulnerabilities.evidence:
URL: https://nmap.org/reset-password
Injected Host: evil.vulnhunter-test.com
Indicator: Reset page reflects poisoned host 'evil.vulnhunter-test.com'
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use a hardcoded base URL for password reset links, not the Host header.

actions: 1. Enable HTTPS on scanme.nmap.org with proper SSL/TLS configuration
2. Fix password reset poisoning by removing Host header dependency
3. Implement SSRF protections with internal IP blocking
1. Implement comprehensive security headers across all domains
2. Fix open redirect vulnerabilities with URL validation
3. Add SPF and DMARC records for email authentication
4. Conduct code review for all user input handling
2. Conduct penetration testing of all subdomains
4. Establish bug bounty program for continuous testing
